title: Glandora prostrata

It is often used as an ornamental plant in rock gardens and other types of landscapes.

intro: Glandora prostrata, also known as Rock Mint or Dwarf Rock Mint, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, fragrant leaves and tiny blue flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Glandora prostrata include essential oils such as menthone, limonene, and beta-pinene, as well as flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Glandora prostrata is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, bronchitis, and other conditions.
